data.title: ref-library-loader.sh pipeline with local-first merge and per-entry LLM judgment
data.description:
As an implementation-plan agent, I can invoke ref-library-loader.sh to annotate generated tasks with up to 6 relevant reference citations from a merged corpus+URL reference set.

Done definitions:
- ref-library-loader.sh calls `dso ref-query --top-n=8` with 30s subprocess timeout and explicit PID tracking (no zombie processes on timeout)
- Calls confluence-fetch.sh per URL reference; caps post-tag-filter set at 12 with consistent budget accounting (failed fetches excluded; retry quota re-filled from next candidate with fixed-point condition: retry until 12 tag-passing references OR exhausted candidate pool OR max-3 retry rounds)
- Merges both sets with local-first precedence + slot-reallocation rule: URL slots expand to fill unused corpus budget ONLY when corpus returns fewer than 8 AND merged-set cap of 20 has remaining capacity
- LLM relevance judgment per-entry (not batched); UTF-8-safe 200-char truncation at Unicode code point boundary (not grapheme cluster, not byte); concurrent-invocation cap of 3
- Selects top 6 entries by relevance score for citation annotation (MAX_CITATIONS_PER_TASK=6)
- Emits citations in URL-sourced OR corpus-sourced format based on entry source
- Unit tests pass

Considerations:
- [Reliability] PID tracking on ref-query child — ensure kill on timeout, no zombies accumulating over N tasks
- [Performance] Concurrent-invocation cap of 3 balances throughput and rate limits
- [Security] No shell injection in task title/description passed to ref-query subprocess or tag filter
- [Testing] Mock LLM client for per-entry judgment
- [Correctness] UTF-8 truncation operates on Unicode code points; never splits multi-byte UTF-8 sequences; fixture includes boundary cases at code-point-N where code point N+1 is multi-byte
- [Reliability] Budget re-fill fixed-point condition prevents infinite retry loop when most candidate URLs fail the tag filter
